title: Construction Companies
excerpt:
Hi, 
Hoping to learn from other construction companies that are using monday. How do you set up your boards, what automations do you use, and what are the most helpful features you utilize for your construction schedules…

posts.author: kate1 (Kate Creamer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

Hoping to learn from other construction companies that are using monday. How do you set up your boards, what automations do you use, and what are the most helpful features you utilize for your construction schedules?

Thanks!

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, @kate1 - There are lots of construction companies using monday, and their board structures are all amazingly different from each other (from what I’ve experienced and who we’ve worked with, at least). The takeaway is that no matter what type of construction your company does - real estate development, residential custom homes, interior office design - monday․com can be customized to suite your exact needs and business processes.

One of the construction case studies in your industry is here: Customer story: Falkbuilt | monday.com which might give you some ideas to start.

If you would like to chat about your vision for using monday․com and do a bit of brainstorming together, we offer a limited number of no-charge strategy sessions each week in the community. You can book yours here: Customer story: Falkbuilt | monday.com

posts.author: kate1 (Kate Creamer)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello @PolishedGeek - Can you please resend the link to book a strategy session? Looks like your second link sends me to the customer story again.

posts.author: ilia510 (Ilia Zelenkin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Kate,

You can also ping @AnthonytheEngineer to get some insight.

Cheers,
Ilia

posts.author: AnthonytheEngineer (Anthony Gude)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you @ilia510 , @kate1 We are a 20 person Architectural and Engineering firm that manages projects as a CM/CA as well. Happy to share what has been a gradual learning curve over the last 6 months. If our structure works for you with daily staff reporting, project costing and schedule management - I am not against handing you a completed structure for you to start.

posts.author: kate1 (Kate Creamer)
posts.cookedHtml: Appreciate it @ilia510!

posts.author: odelegacy (Calvin Ode)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @AnthonytheEngineer , I’ve been using Monday for some basic service work, but we are about to start using it for our construction management. I would be interested in seeing the structure you use. I’ve been slowly tweaking templates, but I think I’ve started to confuse myself with the many ideas/changes I’ve gone through.

posts.author: Jgriffin2440 (James Harold Griffin)
posts.cookedHtml: We are looking for a Monday partner that has the shell built for a residential construction company and willing to work with us to customize / automate the processes. We are new to Monday and this feed looked similar to what we are looking to implement.

posts.author: Crownhomesoflondon (Lisa Banman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Kate. How are you finding Monday now that you’ve been using it for a while. We are a custom building and are looking for the best way to set up.
Thanks,
Lisa

posts.author: kate1 (Kate Creamer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Lisa,

We actually transitioned to BuilderTrend. We found that using a construction management software made more sense than trying to make Monday.com work for us.

Wishing you success!

Kate

posts.author: alimogiannis (Anastasia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Anthony,

If you still have a similar template our team would also love use it as a basis for our 5 person design/ construction team.

Thanks!

posts.author: victor9000 (victor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello @AnthonytheEngineer. Late to the party but I am exploring Monday for our 20-person architectural/construction ADA compliance review services firm. We follow the same general phases like SD-DD-CD and build outs, reviewing drawings and sites at every step Would you be willing to share your Monday template/structure for us to look at and learn from . Options are a bit bewildering for us as we test the platform.

posts.author: saifkhan.760 (Saif khan)
posts.cookedHtml: Use automations to track task dependencies, ensuring that the completion of one task triggers the start of another.

posts.author: PerrySandy (Perry)
posts.cookedHtml: In the construction industry, utilizing construction estimating services is crucial for accurate project planning and budgeting. These services provide precise cost estimates save time through automation and enhance resource management leading to better decision-making and improved client satisfaction. Integrating these tools with project management software like Monday.com can further streamline workflows and improve project outcomes.

posts.author: PerrySandy (Perry)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,
We’re planning to shift our construction management to Monday after using it for basic service tasks. I’m curious about your setup, particularly for managing estimates. Accurate Floridaestimate is key for us, and I’ve been adjusting templates, but the numerous tweaks and ideas are starting to confuse me.

posts.author: reinier (Reinier ― 📅 Calendar Plus 🍅 Automato)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all, our calendar app Calendar Plus comes with weather automations. If your jobs are affected by the weather, have a go! It’s free for two weeks. Don’t hesitate to reach out with any questions or suggestions.

tradespeople-board.60c71e0e1780×445 56.2 KB

posts.author: paisleygenesis (Paisley Genesis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Kate Creamer:

Hoping to learn from other construction companies that are using monday. How do you set up your boards, what automations do you use, and what are the most helpful features you utilize for your construction schedules?

It’s great that you’re looking to optimize your construction management with Monday! A lot of companies find success by setting up separate boards for each project, with columns for tasks, timelines, dependencies, and budgets. Automations like status changes triggering task assignments, or date reminders for approaching deadlines, can really streamline your workflow. One of the most helpful features for construction schedules is the Gantt view, as it lets you visualize project timelines and dependencies easily. How are you currently organizing your boards?

posts.author: Thomas3 (Thomas Jordan)
posts.cookedHtml: Really interesting post! I totally see how estimating services can make a huge difference in project planning. I’m curious—how has using tools like Monday.com changed the way you manage timelines? Do you find it helps when things get hectic and changes come up? Looking forward to hearing more about your experience!

posts.author: LutherCohrs (Luther)
posts.cookedHtml: We use Monday boards to track project phases, timelines, and teams. Automations send reminders and updates. Helpful features include Gantt charts and time tracking. It supports our estimation services too.
